task_completion.description: Open the DreamPlan Home Design Sample House project
task_completion.visual_evidence: The DreamPlan Home Design application is open and displays the built-in Sample House (Contemporary House) project.

task_completion.description: Create a new story/level in the project
task_completion.visual_evidence: The story/level manager, dropdown, or settings dialog shows that a new level has been added to the project.
The newly created story is explicitly named 'Basement' in the UI (e.g., in the level dropdown or story properties dialog).

task_completion.description: Set the elevation of the Basement to a negative value to make it subterranean
task_completion.visual_evidence: The elevation setting for the 'Basement' story shows a negative value (e.g., -8ft, -2.5m) in the story properties or level manager.

task_completion.description: Draw a foundation wall perimeter on the Basement level
task_completion.visual_evidence: Walls are visible in the 2D blueprint or 3D view while the 'Basement' level is the active working level.

task_completion.description: Ensure the basement foundation wall perimeter roughly matches the floor above
task_completion.visual_evidence: The drawn basement walls align with the general footprint/shape of the house above it, often visible by comparing it to the ghosted outline of the upper floor or the overall structure in 3D view.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the DreamPlan Home Design GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active use of the DreamPlan interface, such as open dialog boxes, selected tools, and cursor interactions within the application window.

integrity.description: Agent did not use terminal commands or scripts to bypass the UI
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows or code editors open showing scripts that modify the project file directly.

integrity.description: Agent used the software's built-in drawing tools
integrity.visual_evidence: The wall or foundation building tool is visibly selected in the DreamPlan toolbar during the drawing process.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected name for the new story is exactly 'Basement'. To correctly create a subterranean level in DreamPlan Home Design, the elevation value must be set to a negative number (e.g., -8ft or -2.5m). Look for these specific values in the story/level settings dialog.
